EnhancedContent:
We’re announcing an update to the permissions required to interact with [semantic models](https://aka.ms/Fabric/Data-Agent-SemanticModel-PrepForAI) in Fabric data agents.

Today, the same permission model applies to both creators and consumers of a data agent. To add a semantic model to a data agent, you must have **Member access** to the workspace where the model is stored and **Build permission** on the semantic model.

These same permissions are also required if you simply want to ask questions of a data agent that already includes that semantic model. In short, whether you are creating or using a data agent, you currently need both workspace access and Build permission.

We’re simplifying this experience.

With this update, creators and consumers only need Read permission on the semantic model to interact with it through a data agent. You no longer need workspace access to add a semantic model to a data agent or to ask questions from it. If you have **Read permission**, you can use the semantic model within the data agent experience.

This change applies only to interactions through the data agent. If you need to modify the semantic model or use features such as [Prep for AI](https://aka.ms/PBI/SemanticModel-PrepForAI), you will still need Write permission on the semantic model.

Title: Update to required permissions for Semantic Models in Fabric Data Agents
Author: Microsoft Fabric Blog
